evals.prompt: We need to test our web app against 5 browsers × 4 OS combinations. Should we use BrowserStack, Sauce Labs, or LambdaTest?
evals.expected_output: Should check qa-context.md or ask. Should give a balanced comparison: BrowserStack — broad coverage, mature, expensive; Sauce Labs — strong CI / analytics, enterprise heritage; LambdaTest — usually cheaper, growing fast, less mature ecosystem. For 20 OS×browser combos, all three work. Recommend a 30-day side-by-side pilot of two on the same suite to compare reliability, speed, support quality. Should defer the final pick to qa-context.md (budget, existing relationships, compliance constraints). Should NOT declare a universal winner.
assertions: Reads qa-context.md or asks for it

evals.prompt: Our staging is internal-only behind a VPN. Tests on BrowserStack get DNS errors.
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose: cloud grid can't reach internal hosts without a tunnel. Recommend BrowserStack Local (or Sauce Connect / LambdaTest Tunnel for those providers): run their tunnel binary inside your network, configure the test session with the tunnel identifier. Show the high-level setup pattern. Should mention pinning the tunnel binary version and keeping it running with restart-on-failure (it's a system service in practice). Should warn that some compliance regimes restrict tunnel use — verify with security before deploying.
assertions: Identifies missing tunnel as the cause

evals.prompt: how do i name my browserstack sessions so i can find failures in the dashboard
evals.expected_output: Should treat the casual phrasing as a valid trigger. Should show capability fields: `bstack:options.sessionName` (per-test name) and `bstack:options.buildName` (per-CI-build identifier). Recommend wiring CI variables — `buildName: process.env.GITHUB_RUN_ID` or similar — so every failed session is traceable back to the exact CI run. Should mention `projectName` for filtering by app/repo. Should NOT invent capability names.
assertions: Recommends wiring CI run ID into buildName

evals.prompt: Our mobile testing on AWS Device Farm is super expensive. Should we use Firebase Test Lab instead?
evals.expected_output: Should give a trade-off. AWS Device Farm: real-device-heavy, AWS-native (IAM, VPC), broader device variety, often pricier per minute. Firebase Test Lab: Google-run, real Android + iOS available, simpler pricing for Android, integrates with Google ecosystem. For Android-heavy testing, Firebase often wins on cost. For iOS-heavy testing, real-device options differ — verify current support. Should suggest measuring: monthly minutes used, real-device vs virtual mix, integration with existing CI. Should NOT auto-recommend the cheaper option without context. Cross-reference test-environment-management for cost discipline.
assertions: Notes Android vs iOS support differences

evals.prompt: We're running 4000 real-device tests per month and spending $8K. Where can we cut?
evals.expected_output: Should give cost-discipline levers: (1) audit whether each real-device test really needs a real device — most regression can run on emulators / simulators (1/5 to 1/10 the cost); (2) reserve real devices for the small set that genuinely needs them (biometrics, push, camera, perf-sensitive UX); (3) reduce parallel slot count if utilization is below 60%; (4) timeout discipline — hung sessions waste minutes; (5) cancel-on-new-push in CI so obsolete commits don't run; (6) move full real-device suite to nightly, run smoke (5-10 critical flows) on PR; (7) audit OS/device matrix — drop low-traffic combinations. Should recommend tracking spend monthly and re-auditing. Cross-references test-strategy.

evals.prompt: Should we self-host Selenium Grid instead of paying for BrowserStack?
evals.expected_output: Should give an honest cost analysis: self-hosting saves on per-minute fees but adds infra cost, ops time, monitoring, browser-image maintenance, on-call. Self-host wins when: volume is high (thousands of sessions/day), regulatory constraints forbid cloud, you have idle K8s capacity. Cloud wins when: low-to-medium volume, no spare ops capacity, you need real devices / iOS. Recommend doing the math — estimate annual managed cost vs. annual infra + ops cost. Cross-reference selenium-grid for the self-host evaluation. Should NOT auto-recommend self-hosting.
assertions: Names when self-host wins (volume, compliance, capacity)
Names when cloud wins (low volume, no ops, real devices)
